findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "add_wallet" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Submit a public wallet address for verification. Detects the blockchain, scans on-chain balances, and returns verification options (message signing or dust tran…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Description tells the model to hide behavior
findings.detail: Description of "add_wallet" instructs the model to conceal actions from the user.

findings.title: Anomalously long tool description
findings.detail: Description of "add_wallet" is 2639 chars, far longer than this server's median (417). Overlong descriptions are a common place to bury instructions.

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "add_wallet" stacks urgency/override words (always, never). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: search_assets, get_snippets, get_referral_link, start_identity_verification, add_wallet, remove_wallet, refresh_wallet_verification · Private data: search_assets, get_referral_link, get_supported_crypto, get_account_status, add_wallet, verify_wallet_signature, verify_wallet_transfer, remove_wallet, refresh_wallet_balance, refresh_wallet_verification, get_wallet_summary, generate_proof_of_funds · Public sink: search_assets, get_snippets, add_wallet, verify_wallet_signature, verify_wallet_transfer, refresh_wallet_verification, generate_proof_of_funds · Destructive: add_wallet, remove_wallet, refresh_wallet_verification, get_wallet_summary.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: search_assets, get_snippets, get_referral_link, start_identity_verification, add_wallet, remove_wallet, refresh_wallet_verification · Private data: search_assets, get_referral_link, get_supported_crypto, get_account_status, add_wallet, verify_wallet_signature, verify_wallet_transfer, remove_wallet, refresh_wallet_balance, refresh_wallet_verification, get_wallet_summary, generate_proof_of_funds · Public sink: search_assets, get_snippets, add_wallet, verify_wallet_signature, verify_wallet_transfer, refresh_wallet_verification, generate_proof_of_funds · Destructive: add_wallet, remove_wallet, refresh_wallet_verification, get_wallet_summary

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
